definitions.text: A dark image projected onto a surface where light (or other radiation) is blocked by the shade of an object.
examples: My shadow lengthened as the sun began to set.
The X-ray showed a shadow on his lung.

definitions.text: Relative darkness, especially as caused by the interruption of light; gloom; obscurity.
examples: I immediately jumped into shadow as I saw them approach.

definitions.text: An area protected by an obstacle (likened to an object blocking out sunlight).
examples: The mountains block the passage of rain-producing weather systems and cast a "shadow" of dryness behind them.

definitions.text: Unofficial, informal, unauthorized, but acting as though it were.
examples: The human resources department has a shadow information technology group without headquarters knowledge.

definitions.text: Having power or influence, but not widely known or recognized.
examples: The director has been giving shadow leadership to the other group's project to ensure its success.
The illuminati shadow group has been pulling strings from behind the scenes.

definitions.text: Part of, or related to, the opposition in government.

examples: The artist chose to shadow this corner of the painting.

definitions.text: To block light or radio transmission from.
examples: Looks like that cloud's going to shadow us.

definitions.text: To secretly or discreetly track or follow another, to keep under surveillance.
